title: Why Is Shinji Wet in EoE
display_title: Why Is Shinji Wet in EoE
wikitext:
[]: Why Is Shinji Wet in EoE             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 3:27 am PICS Here we see the far of image And here we see him up close. Why is Shinji wet? Is it possible that the thing(off of the Electrical phone poll) that falls into the ocean splashes him. but that happens after showing him being wet?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Jul 23, 2010 3:50 am If I recall correctly this is the first time we see Shinji in EoE - in which case I'd like to think of it as a cinematic device to show how hurt he is (think 23' and 24'!) and how much he is trying to turn away from that pain: he's insofar into this state that he isn't concerned about being wet, in fact it might be that he drenched himself on purpose for that reason. It's all the more meaningful when we consider the next Shinji scene, the hospital scene, in which he tries to wake Asuka because he needs her / he belives she is the only one who can make him think of something other than his own pain. ^I should note that I only saw EoE once, and at the time I didn't realise it was Shinji that was wet in this scene =<

[]:             Quote Post by BeoX2 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 3:51 am Don't mean to sound rude, but does it really freaking matter? I don't think there's anything philosophical or psychological behind Shinji being "wet."

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 3:54 am BeoX2 wrote: Don't mean to sound rude, but does it really freaking matter? I don't think there's anything philosophical or psychological behind Shinji being "wet."

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Fri Jul 23, 2010 4:02 am I don't think Anno is the sort of person to want to include such a short scene at the very beginning of EoE without it having any value on the story that follows. As for what made him wet, all we're shown is him standing at the edge of the lake that now inhabits the ruins of New Tokyo-3. Since we only see this for about twenty seconds, and Shinji makes no actions whatsoever, the point of the scene is that we see him standing alone . Whatever made him come there is equally ambigious. What we are shown is he isn't staring at the ruins - or at anything - as his hair is explicitly shown to be over his face. He is simply there . [Edit/Update: I realise now that this is the same place where Shinji first met Kaworu. In which case, Shinji selecting this place in particular to be alone follows on from the events of 24'.]

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:14 am It looks like another botched suicide attempt to me. He didn't jump in episode 4, and now he can't figure out drowning.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 5:17 am Yes, especially since his shirt looks dry despite his head being absolutely soaked.

[]:             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Fri Jul 23, 2010 6:54 am Suicide attempt.

[]:             Quote Post by Synapsid » Fri Jul 23, 2010 7:59 am Joseph the PRPD wrote: Suicide attempt.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Jul 23, 2010 8:27 am Suicide is what I figured too, it makes sense because [Unfortunately, I can't recall were this is said, but it is official I believe] Shinji is so weak he can't even kill himself and of course, he can't swim, so you'd figure he's try water. It's significant because if you watch EoE right after episode 24 it takes places in the same area he met Kaworu and were he was last seen in episode 24 with Misato [Perhaps he stayed there all night?] The object hitting the water is pretty symbolic of Kaworu's death as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:00 am Halicat wrote: It's significant because if you watch EoE right after episode 24 it takes places in the same area he met Kaworu and were he was last seen in episode 24 with Misato [Perhaps he stayed there all night?] The object hitting the water is pretty symbolic of Kaworu's death as well.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Jul 23, 2010 11:03 am Azathoth wrote: He must have stayed there all night. Where else can he go? No movie theaters left for him to crash on the couches of.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:10 pm http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8469

[]:             Quote Post by Azathoth » Fri Jul 23, 2010 12:19 pm Ornette wrote: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8469

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Fri Jul 23, 2010 1:00 pm tbh, I just thought he was sweating, seeing how the scene implies that it's effin hot.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 1:05 pm Ornette wrote: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=8469

[]:             Quote Post by Gamer137 » Fri Jul 23, 2010 1:12 pm Is it wrong that I assumed you were asking about the substance on his hand later on? Anyway, it was most likely suicide since he can't swim. He is so depressed at the time that I doubt he was trying to teach himself how, and I doubt it was some kind of spiritual baptism either.

[]:             Quote Post by KLVTZ » Fri Jul 23, 2010 1:25 pm Gamer137 wrote: Is it wrong that I assumed you were asking about the substance on his hand later on? Anyway, it was most likely suicide since he can't swim. He is so depressed at the time that I doubt he was trying to teach himself how, and I doubt it was some kind of spiritual baptism either.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:03 pm Besides the possible suicide attempt, does anyone see that as strangely foreshadowing the hospital scene? Maybe it's just my dirty mind. That said, water is a strong motif in the series and it's always related to origins/returning to the past/mother/Rei/etc.

[]:             Quote Post by Halicat » Fri Jul 23, 2010 9:12 pm Eva Yojimbo wrote: Besides the possible suicide attempt, does anyone see that as strangely foreshadowing the hospital scene? Maybe it's just my dirty mind.
